quantity_needed: per-tier energy per byte moved or retained
reason_deferred: Public product pages rarely expose comparable per-byte energy by tier and workload.
minimum_acceptable_source: vendor power telemetry plus workload-level bytes moved, or peer-reviewed measurement
effect_if_high: Strengthens memory-centric placement if HBM/host/remote movement energy gaps are large.
effect_if_low: Weakens energy claims; leave only latency/capacity arguments.
notes: Do not infer from board TDP.

quantity_needed: real CXL memory latency under contention
model_location: M-QUEUE-1 coordination overhead and CXL tier
reason_deferred: CXL standards expose capability, not deployed p50/p99 latency under multi-tenant pressure.
minimum_acceptable_source: reproducible CXL memory benchmark with contention and topology details
effect_if_high: Can reverse Option B/C if pooled memory adds high queueing delay.
effect_if_low: Makes CXL a stronger spill tier for warm objects.
notes: Capability rows are present; latency is not.

quantity_needed: remote object-store latency distributions for agent state
model_location: M-COST-1 durable tier; M-PROTO-1 durable workspace
reason_deferred: Public storage claims do not map cleanly to agent workspace read/write and replay patterns.
minimum_acceptable_source: trace-backed object-store p50/p95/p99 by object size and consistency mode
effect_if_high: High tails make durable replay and trajectory checkpoints expensive.
effect_if_low: Low tails make pointer/offload paths more attractive.
notes: Must include consistency semantics, not just object size.

model_location: M-COMP-1 safety model; M-PROTO-1 failure cases
reason_deferred: Semantic cache hit rates do not determine correctness under stale, poisoned, or user-isolated contexts.
minimum_acceptable_source: evaluation with cache-hit, false-positive, invalidation, and recovery metrics
effect_if_high: High cost pushes semantic cache toward exact provenance pointers.
effect_if_low: Low cost strengthens Option B for RAG-like workloads.
notes: Security analysis should revisit this in M-SEC-1.

model_location: M-LIFE-1 branch survival; M-QUEUE-1 DAG benefit
reason_deferred: Public serving benchmarks do not expose durable branch/merge/tool-output reuse over long agent runs.
minimum_acceptable_source: instrumented production or open benchmark traces with trajectory DAG events
effect_if_high: High reuse strengthens Option C despite coordination overhead.
effect_if_low: Low reuse collapses many agentic cases toward Option B or A.
notes: Highest-risk missing evidence for the architecture thesis.

reason_deferred: No general public constant covers validating source versions, cache salts, branch lineage, and replay pointers.
minimum_acceptable_source: measured validation microbenchmark plus end-to-end trace replay overhead
effect_if_high: High overhead can erase compression/offload value.
effect_if_low: Low overhead makes pointer-preserving compression safer to deploy.
